categories.description: UTF-8 handling drift can reintroduce mojibake in dashboard/handoff docs.
Operational docs can lag current baseline if not refreshed per task.
Floating refs can drift away from exact SHA truth.
Contracts may reference paths not registered or no longer existing.
PC/VM2 path mismatch can break handoff and apply checks.
Local runtime files can be mistaken for canonical tracked truth.
Using unregistered tools/scripts weakens proof strength.
Legacy continuity areas can flood warnings and hide new regressions.
Capabilities with UNKNOWN install status need explicit warning before execution.
Registry entries may point to missing scripts/tools.
PASS claims without receipts/check evidence are invalid.
Raw advisory must not be treated as accepted doctrine before reconciliation.
Using old baseline head (090c75...) as current truth is stale-risk.
Dirty VM2 worktree before intake can invalidate preflight truth.
